title: Learning
description: The measurement (002). Three falsifiable curves + supporting panels, all via the read-only grafana_ro role (FR-027/029, SC-010). A FLAT escalation curve is a valid negative result (SC-005).

options.content: All current benchmark rows are **contamination_unsafe** (likely in base-model pretraining). Accuracy here is **NOT** a clean generalization claim. A flat escalation curve below is a valid, recorded negative result (recall, not compounding) — SC-005.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('hour', created_at) AS time, avg(cost_usd) AS cost_per_solved_task FROM tasks WHERE verified = true AND $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
SELECT date_trunc('hour', created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN escalated THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS escalation_rate FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

SELECT date_trunc('hour', created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN solve_path = 'retrieval' THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS retrieval_share FROM tasks WHERE solve_path IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1
SELECT count(*) AS episodes FROM short_term_memory
SELECT state, count(*) AS n FROM skills GROUP BY state ORDER BY state
SELECT pool, contamination_safe, count(*) AS n FROM benchmark_tasks GROUP BY pool, contamination_safe ORDER BY pool
SELECT count(*) AS consolidation_runs FROM consolidation_runs
SELECT prediction_error FROM tasks WHERE prediction_error IS NOT NULL
SELECT date_trunc('hour', started_at) AS time, count(*) AS runs, sum(memories_processed) AS memories FROM consolidation_runs WHERE $__timeFilter(started_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

options.content: Daily LLM spend vs the hard cap (`budget_tracking.daily_budget_usd`, Constitution IX) + per-task token/cost. Only escalated (frontier) solves cost; local qwen2.5 + sympy verify are free.

targets.rawSql: SELECT CASE WHEN daily_budget_usd > 0 THEN 100.0 * COALESCE(llm_cost_usd,0) / daily_budget_usd ELSE 0 END AS pct_used FROM budget_tracking WHERE date = CURRENT_DATE
SELECT COALESCE(llm_cost_usd,0) AS spent_today, daily_budget_usd AS cap FROM budget_tracking WHERE date = CURRENT_DATE
SELECT COALESCE(sum(tokens_used),0) AS total_tokens FROM tasks
SELECT COALESCE(sum(llm_cost_usd),0) AS total_spend FROM budget_tracking
SELECT date::timestamptz AS time, COALESCE(llm_cost_usd,0) AS daily_cost, daily_budget_usd AS cap FROM budget_tracking WHERE $__timeFilter(date::timestamptz) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Tokens & cost per escalated task over time

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('hour', created_at) AS time, avg(tokens_used) FILTER (WHERE escalated) AS avg_tokens, avg(cost_usd) FILTER (WHERE escalated) AS avg_cost FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

options.content: Two-stage model — **B (decay)**: effective_salience fades with age (half-life `DECAY_HALFLIFE_DAYS`, stored bookmark immutable). A flat curve is an accepted negative result.

panels.title: Decay — effective_salience vs episode age (003 B, curve b)
panels.description: Each point is a raw episode plotted at its created_at; older episodes (left) show lower effective_salience. Mirrors fenrir.memory.salience.effective_salience() exactly. Anchors live in LTM and are exempt.

targets.rawSql: SELECT created_at AS time, salience * exp(-ln(2) * extract(epoch from (now() - coalesce(last_reactivated_at, created_at))) / (86400 * 7.0)) AS effective_salience FROM short_term_memory WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Replay — abstraction strength vs reinforcement_count (003 C, curve a)
panels.description: Each consolidated abstraction plotted: strength should rise with replay hits (reinforcement_count), not sit at a constant. Flat/absent = no competition yet (valid negative result).

targets.rawSql: SELECT last_reinforced_at AS time, strength, reinforcement_count FROM long_term_memory WHERE memory_type='semantic' AND abstraction_level>=2 AND $__timeFilter(last_reinforced_at) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Reuse — consolidated-abstraction retrieval rate (003 C, curve c — HEADLINE)
panels.description: Share of tasks for which a consolidated long_term_memory abstraction was retrievable/applied (tasks.retrieval_abstraction_id). The SC-008 compounding signal: a rate measurably above zero = consolidated knowledge is being reused. A flat-zero curve is an accepted, honestly-reported negative result.

targets.rawSql: SELECT date_trunc('hour', created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_abstraction_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS abstraction_reuse_rate FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY 1 ORDER BY 1

options.content: Feasibility-gated curriculum (P2.9 / D6) verdict signals, **one point per cohort** (a cohort = one `fenrir-cohort.timer` nightly run, so the daily bucket ≈ one cohort; intra-day manual `--run N` batches merge into that day). Read the four together: **coverage must rise first** (🧩), **reuse must track it** (♻ — accuracy-up + reuse-flat = contamination, not learning, SC-003), **skill-covered escalation must separate below cold** (🎯, SC-002), **overall escalation must trend down** (↓, SC-004). A flat or zero line is a valid, recorded **negative** result (SC-007) — it is drawn plainly, not hidden.

panels.title: 🧩 Coverage per cohort — did a stored skill MATCH? (SC-001)
panels.description: Share of tasks per cohort where a stored skill or consolidated abstraction was retrieved at all (retrieval_skill_id OR retrieval_abstraction_id). The 004 lever's primary target: drag this off ~0.8% so reuse can bite. Must rise before the 🎯 gap below can open.

targets.rawSql: SELECT min(created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_skill_id IS NOT NULL OR retrieval_abstraction_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS coverage FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(cohort_id::text, date_trunc('day',created_at)::text) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: ♻ Reuse tracks coverage? (SC-003 kill cross-check)
panels.description: Coverage (a skill matched) vs reuse (a consolidated abstraction actually drove the solve, retrieval_abstraction_id). Reuse ⊆ coverage. The kill cross-check: if coverage rises but reuse stays flat, the lift is recall/contamination, NOT the compounding library. Reuse must rise in lockstep.

targets.rawSql: SELECT min(created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_skill_id IS NOT NULL OR retrieval_abstraction_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS coverage, avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_abstraction_id IS NOT NULL THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS reuse FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(cohort_id::text, date_trunc('day',created_at)::text) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: 🎯 Skill-covered vs cold escalation — does the gap open? (SC-002)
panels.description: Per cohort: escalation rate on tasks where a skill matched (covered) vs tasks met cold (no stored knowledge). If learning is real, the covered line drops BELOW cold and the gap widens — the skill let it skip the teacher. Lines sitting together = hoarding skills it never reuses (recall, not learning). The kill-switch, per cohort.

targets.rawSql: SELECT min(created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_skill_id IS NOT NULL OR retrieval_abstraction_id IS NOT NULL THEN (CASE WHEN escalated THEN 100.0 ELSE 0.0 END) END) AS "Skill-covered", avg(CASE WHEN retrieval_skill_id IS NULL AND retrieval_abstraction_id IS NULL THEN (CASE WHEN escalated THEN 100.0 ELSE 0.0 END) END) AS "Cold — no skill" FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(cohort_id::text, date_trunc('day',created_at)::text) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: ↓ Overall escalation per cohort (SC-004, flat = negative)
panels.description: Share of all tasks per cohort that needed the expensive teacher. The D7 headline: if compounding is real this trends down over the series. A flat line is the headline failure mode (built recall, not compounding) and is a valid, recorded negative (SC-007).

targets.rawSql: SELECT min(created_at) AS time, avg(CASE WHEN escalated THEN 1.0 ELSE 0.0 END) AS escalation_rate FROM tasks WHERE $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(cohort_id::text, date_trunc('day',created_at)::text) ORDER BY 1

options.content: Reflection effort concentrates on **surprise** (P1.2 / D3). Each task is tiered by prediction-error (escalation forces full): **none** = skip (no model call), **cheap** = a no-LLM note, **full** = one pass → skill edit-or-create. If the gate works, the tier mix tracks the PE distribution (not uniform), and full reflections yield skill **created/edited** events. A cohort with no surprise shows all none/cheap — a valid recorded outcome.

panels.title: Reflection tier mix per cohort (effort tracks surprise)
panels.description: Per-cohort count of tasks at each reflection tier. Concentrated in none/cheap with a thin full slice = the gate is spending effort only where the system was surprised (SC-001/SC-002).

targets.rawSql: SELECT min(created_at) AS time, count(*) FILTER (WHERE reflection_tier='none') AS none, count(*) FILTER (WHERE reflection_tier='cheap') AS cheap, count(*) FILTER (WHERE reflection_tier='full') AS full FROM tasks WHERE is_eval=false AND reflection_tier IS NOT NULL AND $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(cohort_id::text, date_trunc('day',created_at)::text) ORDER BY 1

panels.title: Reflection outcomes per cohort (the compounding payoff)
panels.description: Full-tier outcomes: created (new skill) / edited (new version of a matched skill) / suppressed (budget). created+edited per cohort = surprise turning into library growth (SC-004). Flat zero is a valid recorded negative.

targets.rawSql: SELECT min(created_at) AS time, count(*) FILTER (WHERE reflection_outcome='created') AS created, count(*) FILTER (WHERE reflection_outcome='edited') AS edited, count(*) FILTER (WHERE reflection_outcome='suppressed') AS suppressed FROM tasks WHERE is_eval=false AND $__timeFilter(created_at) GROUP BY COALESCE(cohort_id::text, date_trunc('day',created_at)::text) ORDER BY 1

options.content: Clean contamination-safe family pool (`benchmark='qwen-gen'`). Per solution-method **family**, escalation on the **first** solved member vs **later** members, and reuse on later members. **Compounding ⇔ `escal_rest` < `escal_first` AND `reuse_rest` > 0.** A family where they're equal is **flat = recall**, a valid recorded negative (the cleanest read on the bet, D13).

panels.title: By-family: escalation first vs rest + reuse

targets.rawSql: WITH fam AS (SELECT bt.family, t.escalated, t.retrieval_skill_id, t.retrieval_abstraction_id, row_number() OVER (PARTITION BY bt.family ORDER BY t.created_at) AS pos FROM tasks t JOIN benchmark_tasks bt ON bt.problem_id=t.benchmark_id WHERE bt.benchmark='qwen-gen' AND t.is_eval=false AND t.status<>'in_progress') SELECT family, count(*) AS solved, round(100*avg(escalated::int) FILTER (WHERE pos=1),1) AS escal_first, round(100*avg(escalated::int) FILTER (WHERE pos>1),1) AS escal_rest, round(100*avg((retrieval_skill_id IS NOT NULL OR retrieval_abstraction_id IS NOT NULL)::int) FILTER (WHERE pos>1),1) AS reuse_rest FROM fam GROUP BY family ORDER BY family
